title: Faroe Islands 4K - Cinematic FPV Relaxation Film
creator: Scenic Relaxation
notes: DRAFT CANDIDATE -- produced in a cloud (Cowork) session while the bridge to the user's local machine was intermittently down; not cross-checked against the live exemplars directory for duplicates, not yet written into the repo. Second Faroe Islands candidate in this batch, deliberately from a different creator/format than ecksplorer-faroe-mulafossur-waterfall.json (a single-landmark cinematic piece) to give this location subject/style diversity -- this one is an FPV-genre, whole-archipelago 'relaxation film'. Verified this session via WebFetch of the YouTube oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xl4c2yAVAd4&format=json), which returned title='Faroe Islands 4K - Cinematic FPV Relaxation Film', author_name='Scenic Relaxation', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@ScenicRelaxationFilms', provider_name='YouTube' -- confirms a real, currently-public video with this exact title/creator. The watch-page fetch (for CC-license/upload-date/duration/description) returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) this session, same as the other three candidates in this batch; not retried further or worked around, so `license_category` is the conservative all-rights-reserved default on absence of a positive CC finding, not a directly confirmed one -- re-check when reachable. A WebSearch this session ('"Scenic Relaxation" youtube channel drone films subscribers') found materially stronger corroboration than the other three candidates: a dedicated channel website (scenicrelaxationvideos.com), a cross-posting relationship with a second channel ('Destination Paradise', per a press item about a Canada film), and listings on multiple independent YouTube-analytics trackers (NoxInfluencer, vidIQ, Social Blade) -- analytics services generally only track channels with meaningful, measurable audiences, so this is real (if imprecise) evidence of an established, prolific, multi-country dedicated relaxation/travel-drone content operation, not a one-off upload. No exact subscriber figure was directly read from any of those pages this session, so none is stated. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: no contest/broadcaster/festival attribution found. `scores` are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated`, calibrated as the strongest-corroborated candidate in this batch -- placed close to pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json's established-FPV-creator tier (0.77/0.76/0.82) but very slightly below it, since pshepfpv's record carries a directly-confirmed large following (474K/1.1M) whereas this channel's reach is corroborated only via third-party trackers, not a first-hand follower count. `composite_score` is not a call to the repo's real `compute_composite_score` (no shell access to the user's environment this session); it was computed via an equivalent equal-weighted-mean formula run in this session's own Python: (0.74+0.75+0.78)/3 = 0.7566666666666667, rounded to 0.7567. `composition` stays null.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not independently reviewed via playback this session. The title's own 'Relaxation Film' framing implies deliberately slow, smooth, contemplative pacing (consistent with the relatively high motion_smoothness estimate above), which is a text-provenance inference, not a genuine cut/shot-length observation -- correct if a live-playback review finds otherwise.
